---
format: html
title: save_prediction_animation()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>save_prediction_animation</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Save Prediction Animation</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Renders one prediction variable across all age slices, saves individual
PNG frames, and combines them into a GIF animation.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>save_prediction_animation(
  data_frame,
  value_column,
  subtitle_label,
  fill_label,
  fill_limits,
  vec_age_slices,
  path_output,
  list_prediction_grid,
  data_world,
  grid_resolution,
  time_step,
  fill_trans = scales::transform_identity(),
  fill_colors = NULL,
  data_points = NULL,
  point_color = "#ff4d4d",
  metric_label = NULL,
  frame_directory_name,
  output_file_name,
  vec_palette = NULL,
  font_family = "VT323"
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="data_frame">data_frame</code></td>
<td>
<p>Tibble with columns 'age', 'coord_long', 'coord_lat', and the column
named by 'value_column'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="value_column">value_column</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar. Name of the fill value column.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="subtitle_label">subtitle_label</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar. Plot subtitle.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="fill_label">fill_label</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar. Legend title.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="fill_limits">fill_limits</code></td>
<td>
<p>Numeric vector of length 2. Fill scale limits.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="vec_age_slices">vec_age_slices</code></td>
<td>
<p>Numeric vector of age values, one per animation frame.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="path_output">path_output</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar. Root output directory for frames and GIF.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="list_prediction_grid">list_prediction_grid</code></td>
<td>
<p>Named list with 'x_lim' and 'y_lim' entries.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="data_world">data_world</code></td>
<td>
<p>World polygon tibble with columns 'long', 'lat', and 'group'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="grid_resolution">grid_resolution</code></td>
<td>
<p>Numeric scalar. Tile width and height in degrees.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="time_step">time_step</code></td>
<td>
<p>Numeric scalar. Half-window in years for matching 'data_points' ages
to each frame age.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="fill_trans">fill_trans</code></td>
<td>
<p>Scale transform passed to 'ggplot2::scale_fill_gradientn()'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="fill_colors">fill_colors</code></td>
<td>
<p>Optional character vector of fill gradient colours.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="data_points">data_points</code></td>
<td>
<p>Optional tibble with 'age', 'coord_long', 'coord_lat' for overlaid
observation points.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="point_color">point_color</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar. Colour for observation points.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="metric_label">metric_label</code></td>
<td>
<p>Optional character vector appended as a plot caption.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="frame_directory_name">frame_directory_name</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar. Name of the sub-directory for PNG frames.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="output_file_name">output_file_name</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar. Output GIF file name.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="vec_palette">vec_palette</code></td>
<td>
<p>Optional named character vector of ORACLE colours. If 'NULL',
colours are read with 'get_oracle_palette_values()'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="font_family">font_family</code></td>
<td>
<p>Character scalar. Font family for all text elements.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>List returned by [build_gif_from_frames()] with 'animation_path' and
'used_magick' entries.
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
